comment.body:
And I tell you again...
Always be patient, and avoid trading for only the sake of it.
Without conception, without plan, without thinking, there is no longer-term growth. 
Be patient with the growth.
Market won't let you to be millionaire overnight.

comment.body:
Most ppl fall for the mistake to try to trade everything like if somebody would put a gun to your head to do. But if I would ask, why you made this or that decision, you could not answer properly.
This is a mental trap. I tell you what I mean by that.
You have $800 on your account. You go trading and you lose $300
Next day you win again $500
Next day you lose $200, but you get a successful 'revenge trading' and win $400
You get overconfident and lose $700 on the next day. 
And finally on the last day you can gain $300.

Now listen...
 -300 + 500 - 200 + 400 - 700 + 300 = 0

Think about it...

comment.body: Trading isn’t about “who’s right” or some battle of bulls versus bears—shorting the bull trap and longing the bear trap just to feel superior. That kind of behavior comes at a cost. If you lose sight of what’s really at stake in trading, your broker will sure remind you.
comment.raw_body: Trading isn’t about “who’s right” or some battle of bulls versus bears—shorting the bull trap and longing the bear trap just to feel superior. That kind of behavior comes at a cost. If you lose sight of what’s really at stake in trading, your broker will sure remind you.

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
In a rangebound market, if you can’t fade the momentum in time you’ll always be late. That’s why I say you’ve got a better edge on the short-vol side. If you miss a move, it’s smarter to sit it out—your cash doesn’t need to be working 24/7, especially when risk is high.
And when a clear trend’s in place, don’t try to fade it. It won’t make you any smarter—just a less wealthier.
Trading without a solid strategy and understanding is an expensive pastime. Trust me, I know...

Trading without a solid strategy and understanding is an expensive pastime. Trust me, I know...

comment.body:
🌀 Chop Zone: 5610–5660
Pre-FOMC balance zone
Expect compression until 2:00 PM
Minor drift or false breaks possible, but real direction waits on Powell
Still contained unless vol shifts or headline breaks early
🔼 IF SPX Pushes Above 5660 →
5675 → Initial expansion shelf
First test of upside traction
THEN → 5690–5695 → Stall / Battle Line
Key pivot within structure — reactions likely here IF post-FOMC tone is positive or neutral and we’re above 5640/50 (the higher the better)
Risk of fade increases if we pause here post-rally
THEN → 5700 / 5725 → Primary magnets on the map
Largest structural features
5700 = round number, recent swing, dealer focus
5725 = high congestion gamma shelf, past inflection
Expect reactions at both unless momentum overwhelms
THEN → 5735+ → Thin air
Acceleration zone only on strong post-FOMC chase
Late-day upside tail if breakout builds
🔽 IF SPX Breaks Below 5610 →
5580–5560 → Fast flush zone
Opens quickly on hawkish interpretation
THEN → 5540–5535 → Structural inflection
Gamma trough and major test level
THEN → 5500–5490 → Tail risk
Extreme scenario if risk-off accelerates or Powell delivers a shock
📊 Extremes to Watch
>5735 = Squeeze extension / momentum breakout
<5490 = Breakdown tail risk
🔁 Key Zone Summary
5610–5660 → Chop Zone
5675 → Expansion shelf
5690–5695 → Battle Line (key intraday pivot)
5700 / 5725 → Primary upside magnets
5540–5535 → Structural support
5500 → Panic floor
Let the post-2:00 PM reaction confirm which roadmap activates.
